title: Berlin Restaurant Phrases - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin restaurant phrases are most useful when they are short.** Learn one ordering line, one question line, one allergy line and one payment line instead of memorizing a whole phrasebook.
**Use "ich hätte gern..." for polite ordering** in cafes, bakeries, snack bars and relaxed restaurants. It sounds softer than a bare "I want" translation.
**For allergies, show the written German sentence and ask staff to check.** EU rules require allergen information for restaurants and cafes, but you still need a clear conversation before ordering.
**Cash still matters in some Berlin food situations.** Ask "Kann ich mit Karte zahlen?" before assuming a small bakery, snack bar or older restaurant takes cards.
**For paying, "Die Rechnung bitte" gets the bill and "Stimmt so" means keep the change.** Say the final rounded amount when you want to tip in cash.
